frameworks.name: deep learning preprocessing pipeline
frameworks.one_line: deep-learning modules replace classical steps of neuroimaging preprocessing.

frameworks.name: workflow manager orchestration
frameworks.one_line: workflow manager scales pipeline execution across large neuroimaging cohorts.

frameworks.name: state-of-the-art classical MRI pipeline
frameworks.one_line: benchmark pipeline against which DeepPrep's speed and robustness are measured.

core_question: Can a deep-learning-empowered pipeline preprocess large-scale structural and functional MRI data with markedly higher speed, scalability, and robustness than the current state of the art?
key_assumptions: deep-learning modules can substitute classical steps (skull-stripping, registration, normalization, BOLD denoising)
a workflow manager is necessary to scale across tens of thousands of scans
multi-site and clinical cohorts are valid stress tests of robustness
manipulations: cohort type (healthy volunteers, multi-site replications, clinical datasets)
key_findings: DeepPrep achieves roughly tenfold acceleration relative to the state-of-the-art pipeline
DeepPrep scales to >55,000 scans without loss of throughput
DeepPrep remains robust across healthy, multi-site, and clinical cohorts
interpretations: deep-learning-empowered preprocessing meets the scalability demands of big-data neuroimaging
deep-learning modules can replace classical steps for structural and functional MRI preprocessing
